name: Public Health and Human Services Subpack
description: Vertical-specialized value pack for Medicaid administration, public health departments, child welfare, unemployment insurance, SNAP/benefits, housing assistance, behavioral health, aging/disability/veterans services, and disease surveillance. Provides domain-specific pains, KPIs, formulas, benchmarks, signal rules, personas, and buying triggers for B2G sales and value engineering in health and human services agencies.
verticalFocus: State and Local Public Health Departments

pains.name: Medicaid Eligibility Redetermination Backlog >90 Days
pains.description: Post-COVID unwinding and ongoing redetermination cycles create massive backlogs. States face CMS compliance risk and coverage gaps for vulnerable populations when eligibility determinations exceed statutory timeframes.
symptoms: Call center hold times >30 min during renewal waves
sources: KFF Medicaid Enrollment and Unwinding Tracker 2024

pains.name: SNAP Error Rate >6%
pains.description: SNAP Quality Control (QC) error rates above federal thresholds trigger enhanced oversight, reduced federal funding share, and corrective action requirements. Root causes include outdated eligibility systems, insufficient verification, and worker errors.
symptoms: High worker turnover in eligibility units
sources: USDA SNAP QC Annual Report FY2023
FNS State SNAP Error Rate Reports

pains.name: Child Welfare Case Management Data Fragmentation
pains.description: Child welfare agencies lack unified case management across intake, investigation, foster care, adoption, and family services. Caseworkers manage 15+ separate systems, causing data gaps that endanger child safety and trigger audit findings.
sources: HHS ACF Child Welfare Outcomes Reports

pains.name: Unemployment Insurance Claims Backlog and Fraud Surge
pains.description: UI agencies face conflicting pressures: clear pandemic-era backlogs while maintaining program integrity. Identity theft and organized fraud rings exploited weaknesses, leading to $100B+ in estimated improper payments nationally.

pains.name: Public Health Disease Surveillance Data Latency
pains.description: Disease surveillance relies on fragmented, manual reporting pathways. COVID-19 exposed critical gaps: labs faxing results, inconsistent EHR extraction, and week-long delays in outbreak detection. Federal mandates (e.g., CDC Data Modernization Initiative) require transformation.
symptoms: Reportable disease notification >7 days from event
Manual data entry from faxed lab reports
affectedSubSegments: State and Local Public Health Departments

pains.name: Behavioral Health Access Gap and Provider Shortage
pains.description: Behavioral health programs face severe provider shortages, long wait times, and disjointed care pathways. SAMHSA block grant and Medicaid reimbursement constraints limit expansion. Crisis systems (988, mobile crisis) require rapid scale-up.
symptoms: Wait time for outpatient behavioral health >30 days
sources: SAMHSA National Survey on Drug Use and Health

pains.name: Housing Assistance Voucher Waitlist Backlog
pains.description: Public Housing Authorities maintain voucher waitlists of 2-8 years. Administrative burden of HQS inspections, landlord engagement, and portability processing limits throughput. HOME and LIHTC program administration adds complexity.

pains.name: Medicaid Managed Care Oversight and Performance Gaps
pains.description: States delegate 80%+ of Medicaid beneficiaries to MCOs but struggle with oversight: incomplete encounter data, quality metric gaps, network adequacy complaints, and rate-setting disputes. CMS requires state-directed payments and equity analyses.
symptoms: MCO quality ratings below state benchmark
sources: CMS Medicaid Managed Care Final Rule 2024

pains.name: Aging and Disability Services Waitlist for Long-Term Care
pains.description: State ADRCs and No Wrong Door systems face demand exceeding capacity. Older adults and people with disabilities wait months for home and community-based services (HCBS), risking institutionalization. Olmstead compliance pressures mount.

pains.name: Veterans Benefits and Services Coordination Gaps
pains.description: State veterans agencies struggle to coordinate with federal VA, county veterans service officers, and community providers. Benefits claims backlog, mental health access, and housing navigation require integrated case management.
symptoms: State veterans benefits claims backlog >90 days
No shared case file with federal VA
Veteran homelessness rate above state average

pains.name: Eligibility Worker Productivity and Retention Crisis
pains.description: High turnover (>25% annually in many states), insufficient training, and archaic systems combine to crush eligibility worker productivity. Each vacancy costs $15K-$50K to backfill and delays benefit delivery.

pains.name: TANF Caseload Decline vs. Administrative Cost Rigidity
pains.description: TANF cash assistance caseloads declined 70%+ since 1996, but administrative infrastructure costs remain fixed. States face pressure to redirect TANF funds to work supports, child care, and services while maintaining compliance.

pains.name: MMIS (Medicaid Management Information System) End-of-Life
pains.description: Many states operate MMIS systems built in the 1990s-2000s on outdated technology. CMS requires modularity, MITA alignment, and cloud-readiness. Replacement costs run $200M-$500M and take 3-5 years.
symptoms: MMIS vendor support contract ending <24 months
sources: Medicaid and CHIP Payment Advisory Commission

pains.name: Immunization Registry Interoperability and Coverage Gaps
pains.description: IIS (Immunization Information Systems) lack bidirectional data exchange with EHRs, pharmacies, and federal systems (VAMS, IZ Gateway). COVID-19 highlighted gaps in adult immunization tracking and cross-jurisdictional sharing.
affectedSubSegments: State and Local Public Health Departments

pains.name: Human Services Data Integration Failure (Cross-Program)
pains.description: Benefit programs (Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, LIHEAP, WIC) operate on separate eligibility systems. Families re-verify income, residency, and household composition multiple times. No unified beneficiary view exists.
symptoms: Families submitting same documents to >3 programs
Income verification conducted independently per program
Ex parte renewal limited to single program
sources: Code for America Integrated Benefits Research

pains.name: Emergency Preparedness and Health Response Coordination
pains.description: Public health emergency preparedness (PHEP) funding has declined in real terms. Coordination between state health departments, emergency management, hospitals, and EMS remains fragmented. SNS and MCM distribution plans lack regular exercise.
symptoms: No joint exercise with emergency management >24 months
affectedSubSegments: State and Local Public Health Departments

pains.name: Child Support Enforcement System Modernization Lag
pains.description: State CSE systems built under federal IV-D requirements face modernization gaps. Intergovernmental case processing, employer interface, and collections tracking require upgrades. Federal incentive funding tied to performance.
sources: Federal OCSE Annual Report to Congress

pains.name: CCDF Child Care Subsidy Administration Complexity
pains.description: Child Care and Development Fund (CCDF) administration requires complex eligibility, provider enrollment, attendance tracking, and quality rating integration. States face reauthorization compliance and parent copayment collection challenges.

kpis.name: Medicaid Eligibility Determination Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Determination Date - Application Date) for applications resolved in period
kpis.benchmarkRange: Federal max: 45 days (90 for disability); best practice: <30 days

kpis.name: Ex Parte Renewal Rate
kpis.formula: Ex Parte Renewals Completed / Total Renewals Due * 100%

kpis.name: Medicaid Churn Rate
kpis.formula: (Disenrollments + Re-enrollments in period) / Average Enrollment * 100%

kpis.name: SNAP QC Error Rate
kpis.formula: Dollar Error Cases / Total QC Review Cases * 100%

kpis.name: SNAP Application Processing Timeliness
kpis.formula: Applications Processed Within 30 Days / Total Applications Resolved * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Federal requirement: >90% within 30 days; expedited: <7 days

kpis.name: Child Welfare CPS Response Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Initial Contact Date - Report Receipt Date) for screened-in reports
kpis.benchmarkRange: Immediate risk: <1 hour; high risk: <24 hours; best practice: <50% of statutory limit

kpis.name: AFCARS Data Quality Rate
kpis.formula: (1 - AFCARS Error Elements / Total Elements Submitted) * 100%

kpis.name: Recurrence of Maltreatment Rate
kpis.formula: Children with Substantiated Re-report Within 12 Months / Children with Initial Substantiation * 100%

kpis.name: UI First Payment Timeliness
kpis.formula: First Payments Within 14/21 Days of Filing / Total First Payments * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Federal target: >87% within 21 days (14 for non-UI); best practice: >90%

kpis.name: UI Improper Payment Rate
kpis.formula: (Improper UI Payments / Total UI Payments) * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Federal target: <10%; pandemic peak: >15%; best practice: <5%

kpis.name: UI Identity Verification Resolution Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Verification Complete Date - Hold Start Date) for identity holds

kpis.name: Notifiable Disease Reporting Timeliness
kpis.formula: Reports Submitted Within Required Timeframe / Total Notifiable Events * 100%

kpis.name: ELR (Electronic Lab Reporting) Coverage
kpis.formula: Labs Reporting Electronically / Total Reporting Labs * 100%

kpis.name: Behavioral Health Wait Time for Outpatient Services
kpis.formula: AVG(Appointment Date - Request Date) for outpatient non-crisis

kpis.name: 988 Crisis Call Answer Rate
kpis.formula: Crisis Calls Answered / Total Crisis Calls * 100%

kpis.name: Behavioral Health Integration with Primary Care
kpis.formula: Co-located or Integrated BH-PC Practices / Total Primary Care Practices in Network * 100%

kpis.name: Housing Voucher Lease-Up Rate
kpis.formula: Vouchers Leased / Total Vouchers Allocated * 100%

kpis.name: HQS Inspection Pass Rate on First Inspection
kpis.formula: Units Passing HQS on First Inspection / Total HQS Inspections * 100%

kpis.name: Medicaid MCO Encounter Data Completeness
kpis.formula: Encounters with Complete Required Data Elements / Total Encounters Submitted * 100%

kpis.name: MCO Network Adequacy Ratio
kpis.formula: Available Providers per 1,000 Enrollees / CMS Network Adequacy Standard * 100%

kpis.name: HCBS Waiver Waitlist Length
kpis.formula: Individuals on HCBS Waiver Waitlist / Total HCBS Waiver Capacity * 100%

kpis.name: ADRC No Wrong Door Contact Resolution Rate
kpis.formula: Contacts Resolved Without Transfer / Total ADRC Contacts * 100%

kpis.name: Veterans Benefits Claims Processing Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Determination Date - Application Date) for state veterans benefits claims

kpis.name: Veteran Mental Health Referral Completion Rate
kpis.formula: Referrals Completed / Total Referrals * 100%

kpis.name: Eligibility Worker Turnover Rate
kpis.formula: Workers Separated / Average Worker Headcount * 100%

kpis.name: Eligibility Worker Time-to-Productivity
kpis.formula: AVG(Date of Independent Case Processing - Hire Date)

kpis.name: TANF Work Participation Rate
kpis.formula: TANF Participants Meeting Work Requirements / Total TANF Participants * 100%

kpis.name: TANF Administrative Cost Per Case
kpis.formula: Total TANF Administrative Costs / Average Monthly TANF Caseload

kpis.name: MMIS Claim Adjudication Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Adjudication Complete Date - Claim Receipt Date)

kpis.name: MMIS System Uptime
kpis.formula: (Scheduled Uptime - Unplanned Downtime) / Scheduled Uptime * 100%

kpis.name: IIS-EHR Bidirectional Interface Coverage
kpis.formula: Providers with Active Bidirectional IIS Interface / Total Providers * 100%

kpis.name: IIS Adult Immunization Completeness
kpis.formula: Adult Patients with Complete Immunization History / Total Adult Patients in IIS * 100%

kpis.name: Cross-Program Duplicate Verification Rate
kpis.formula: Duplicate Verifications Conducted / Total Verifications * 100%

kpis.name: Integrated Eligibility System Coverage
kpis.formula: Programs on Unified Eligibility Platform / Total Benefit Programs * 100%

kpis.name: PHEP Grant Drawdown Rate
kpis.formula: PHEP Funds Drawn Down / Total PHEP Award * 100%

kpis.name: Joint Public Health-Emergency Management Exercise Rate
kpis.formula: Joint Exercises Conducted / Target Exercises * 100%

kpis.name: Child Support Collections Rate
kpis.formula: Collections Received / Total Obligations * 100%

kpis.name: Interstate Child Support Case Processing Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Case Resolution Date - Interstate Referral Date)

kpis.name: CCDF Eligibility Determination Timeliness
kpis.formula: CCDF Applications Processed Within 30 Days / Total Applications * 100%

kpis.name: CCDF Provider Reimbursement Cycle Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Payment Date - Submission Date) for provider reimbursement claims

governance.reviewOwner: Vertical Subpack Architect — Public Health & Human Services

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Eligibility determination time >45 days; ex parte rate <40%; churn >15%
SNAP QC error rate >6%; USDA corrective action pending; worker turnover >25%
Caseworker accessing >10 systems; AFCARS errors >5%; recurrence of maltreatment >10%
UI claims pending >21 days >10%; overpayment balance growing; identity hold queue >30 days
Disease report notification >7 days; manual faxed lab reports; syndromic surveillance coverage <80%
Behavioral health wait time >30 days; psychiatrist vacancy >20%; crisis call abandonment >5%
Housing voucher waitlist >5,000; wait time >24 months; landlord participation declining
MCO encounter completeness <85%; network adequacy complaints up; HEDIS scores declining
HCBS waitlist >12 months; nursing facility diversion <50%; Olmstead plan citation
Eligibility worker turnover >25%; time-to-productivity >6 months; caseload >statutory max
MMIS vendor contract ending <24 months; claim adjudication >30 days; no API layer
Families submitting same docs to >3 programs; no cross-program rules engine; duplicate benefits >2%

formulas.name: Medicaid Churn Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if state administrative data available; MEDIUM if using KFF estimates; LOW if enrollment unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: 18%->8% churn on 800K enrollees, $120/admin cost, $180 gap cost: 80K * $300 = $24M annual

formulas.name: Ex Parte Renewal Efficiency Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if eligibility system data available; MEDIUM if using state averages; LOW if no renewal volume
formulas.exampleCalculation: 150K renewals shifted, $30 manual cost, $2M churn value, $500K compliance: $4.5M + $2M + $500K = $7M

formulas.name: SNAP Error Rate Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if USDA QC data available; MEDIUM if using state average; LOW if outlay unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: $500M outlay, 8%->4% error, 50% federal share, $1M corrective action: $500M * 0.04 * 0.5 + $1M = $11M

formulas.name: Child Welfare Case Management Consolidation Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = Systems_Consolidated * Maintenance_Cost_Per_System + Caseworker_Hours_Saved * Loaded_Rate + Recurrence_Reduction_Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if system and staffing data available; MEDIUM if estimated; LOW if no case volume

formulas.name: UI Fraud and Improper Payment Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if DOL ETA data available; MEDIUM if using GAO estimates; LOW if payment volume unknown

formulas.name: Disease Surveillance Modernization Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if time-motion and outbreak cost data available; MEDIUM if using CDC estimates; LOW if baseline unknown

formulas.name: Behavioral Health Access Expansion Value
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM if provider network and claims data available; LOW if patient volume unknown

formulas.name: Housing Voucher Throughput Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if PHA data available; MEDIUM if using HUD averages; LOW if no voucher count

formulas.name: MCO Encounter Data Improvement Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if MCO data available; MEDIUM if using CMS estimates; LOW if encounter volume unknown

formulas.name: HCBS Waitlist Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if state HCBS and nursing facility cost data available; MEDIUM if using KFF estimates; LOW if waitlist unknown

formulas.name: Eligibility Worker Retention Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = Turnover_Reduction_Count * (Recruitment_Cost + Training_Cost + Productivity_Loss_Cost) + Overtime_Reduction + Backlog_Cost_Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if HRIS and payroll data available; MEDIUM if using industry estimates; LOW if no turnover data

formulas.name: Cross-Program Eligibility Integration Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = Duplicate_Verification_Hours_Eliminated * Labor_Rate + Error_Reduction_Value + Citizen_Satisfaction_Improvement_Value + ExParte_Expansion_Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if cross-program time-motion data available; MEDIUM if estimated; LOW if no integration scope defined

benchmarks.name: SNAP National QC Error Rate
benchmarks.source: USDA SNAP QC Annual Report FY2023

benchmarks.name: Child Welfare CPS Response Time (High Risk)
Behavioral Health Wait Time (Median)
benchmarks.source: SAMHSA; NAMI State Mental Health Report

benchmarks.name: 988 Crisis Line Answer Rate (National)
benchmarks.source: SAMHSA 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline Data

benchmarks.name: PHA HQS First Inspection Pass Rate
ELR Coverage (National)
benchmarks.source: CDC ELR Data; ONC Interoperability Metrics

benchmarks.name: Eligibility Worker Turnover (Human Services)
benchmarks.source: APHSA Workforce Survey; State HHR Data

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Eligibility determination time >45 days growing 10%+ MoM; ex parte renewal rate <40%; CMS corrective action plan issued
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: State eligibility systems and staffing cannot handle redetermination volume; CMS compliance risk; coverage gap crisis for vulnerable populations

signalRules.signalName: SNAP QC Error Rate Above Threshold
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SNAP QC error rate >6% for 2+ quarters; USDA corrective action notice; worker training completion <70%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Eligibility determination accuracy declining; federal funding at risk; worker competency and system verification gaps

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Caseworkers using >10 systems; AFCARS error rate >5%; recurrence of maltreatment >10%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Child safety data scattered across silos; case history gaps endanger children; federal compliance failing; worker productivity crushed by system switching

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Claims pending >21 days >10% of volume; identity verification queue >30 days; overpayment balance growing >$500M
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: UI system overwhelmed by volume and fraud prevention requirements; trust fund solvency at risk; claimant experience severely degraded

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Notifiable disease reporting >7 days; manual fax/phone lab reporting observed; syndromic surveillance coverage <80% of EDs
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Outbreak detection delayed by days to weeks; CDC DMI mandate unfulfilled; EHR interoperability gaps; public health risk elevated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Outpatient wait time >30 days; psychiatrist vacancy rate >20%; 988 call abandonment >5%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Provider shortage and system capacity inadequate; crisis diversion to ED and law enforcement; SAMHSA block grant requirements unmet

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Lease-up rate <90%; waitlist growing >10% annually; landlord participation rate declining
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: PHA administrative capacity insufficient; inspection and processing bottlenecks; affordable housing crisis worsening

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: MCO encounter completeness <85%; network adequacy complaints increasing; HEDIS scores below 50th percentile
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: State MCO oversight infrastructure inadequate; beneficiary access at risk; CMS 438 subpart D compliance gaps; rate-setting data unreliable

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: HCBS waiver waitlist >12 months; nursing facility admissions increasing; Olmstead plan citation or settlement
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Community-based services capacity insufficient; institutionalization preference growing; CMS enforcement risk; civil rights compliance at stake

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Turnover >25% annually; time-to-productivity >6 months; caseload per worker >statutory maximum
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Human services workforce in crisis; training investment lost; service quality and timeliness severely degraded

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: MMIS contract ending <24 months; claim adjudication >30 days; no API or interoperability layer; CMS MITA <Level 3
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Medicaid payment system at end of life; vendor lock-in risk; modernization required for CMS compliance; $200M+ replacement pending

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Families submitting same documents to >3 programs; no cross-program income verification; duplicate benefits detected
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Program-centric systems creating administrative waste and beneficiary burden; integrated eligibility not implemented; federal streamlined verification mandate unfulfilled

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PHEP grant drawdown <80%; HPP hospital coalition participation <70%; SNS inventory rotation backlog; no joint exercise >24 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Emergency preparedness funding underutilized; coordination systems inadequate; next pandemic or bioterror event response at risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: IIS-EHR bidirectional interface <60%; adult immunization completeness <50%; cross-jurisdictional sharing <30%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Immunization tracking inadequate for adult and cross-state populations; EHR integration gaps; CDC IIS functional standards unmet

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: TANF caseload <25% of 1996 level; admin cost per case >$500/month; work participation rate <50%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Fixed administrative infrastructure serving shrinking caseload; TANF funds not redirected to services; federal compliance at risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Collections rate <55%; IV-D system on unsupported platform; interstate processing >180 days
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Technology and process gaps limiting child support collections; federal incentive funding at risk; families not receiving support

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CCDF eligibility determination >30 days; provider reimbursement delay >45 days; attendance tracking paper-based
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Child care subsidy delivery inefficient; provider cash flow strained; parent employment at risk; CCDF reauthorization requirements unmet

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: State veterans claims backlog >90 days; no data sharing with federal VA; veteran homelessness rate above state average
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: State-federal veterans coordination broken; benefits not reaching veterans; mental health and housing navigation siloed

evidenceSources.name: CMS Medicaid Data (T-MSIS, MSIS, CMS-64)
evidenceSources.description: Medicaid enrollment, claims, financial management, and eligibility data at state and federal levels.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: CMS.gov; state Medicaid agency data requests

evidenceSources.name: KFF (Kaiser Family Foundation) Medicaid/Health Policy
evidenceSources.description: Non-partisan health policy research with state-level Medicaid data, enrollment trends, and financing analyses.

evidenceSources.name: USDA SNAP QC and State Performance Data
evidenceSources.description: SNAP Quality Control error rates, timeliness, and state-level performance metrics.

evidenceSources.name: DOL ETA UI Performance Data (901/902 Reports)
evidenceSources.description: Unemployment insurance claims processing, timeliness, overpayments, and trust fund data.

evidenceSources.name: HHS ACF Child Welfare Data (AFCARS, NCANDS)
evidenceSources.description: Adoption and Foster Care Analysis Reporting System and National Child Abuse and Neglect Data System.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: ACF.HHS.gov; state child welfare agency data

evidenceSources.name: CDC Surveillance and DMI Data
evidenceSources.description: Disease surveillance, notifiable disease data, electronic lab reporting, and Data Modernization Initiative metrics.

evidenceSources.name: SAMHSA Behavioral Health Block Grant Reports
evidenceSources.description: Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant and Community Mental Health Services Block Grant data.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: SAMHSA.gov; state behavioral health agency reports

evidenceSources.name: HUD Housing Program Data (VMS, Picture, PHA Plans)
evidenceSources.description: Housing voucher utilization, PHA performance, and subsidized household demographics.

evidenceSources.name: CMS MITA and MMIS Procurement Data
evidenceSources.description: Medicaid Information Technology Architecture assessments and state MMIS procurement records.

evidenceSources.name: MACPAC (Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission)
evidenceSources.description: Non-partisan federal advisory body on Medicaid and CHIP policy, payment, and access.
evidenceSources.updateFrequency: Quarterly reports; annual report to Congress

buyingTriggers.name: Medicaid Redetermination / Unwinding Wave
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS unwinding deadline approaching; state redetermination backlog crisis; CMS corrective action plan issued
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Expedited eligibility system procurement; call center expansion; ex parte automation; staff augmentation

buyingTriggers.name: SNAP QC Error Rate Penalty
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: USDA issues enhanced oversight notice; federal funding share reduction threatened; corrective action plan required
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Eligibility system upgrades; worker training technology; verification automation; quality monitoring tools

buyingTriggers.name: CMS Medicaid Managed Care Final Rule Compliance
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS publishes updated 438 final rule with network adequacy, encounter data, and rate-setting requirements
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: MCO oversight platform; encounter data validation; network adequacy monitoring; equity analysis tools

buyingTriggers.name: MMIS Contract Recompete / End-of-Life
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: MMIS vendor contract within 18 months of expiration; CMS requires modular replacement; MITA maturity gap documented
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Multi-hundred-million RFP; modular MMIS procurement; claims processing replacement; interoperability platform

buyingTriggers.name: UI System Modernization Mandate (DOL)
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: DOL ETA issues UI modernization guidance; trust fund insolvency risk; fraud detection requirements
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: UI benefits system replacement; identity verification platform; fraud detection analytics; cloud migration

buyingTriggers.name: Child Welfare Case Management RFP
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: AFCARS compliance failure; state court order; Title IV-E foster care reform; CFSR outcome improvement plan
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Unified case management system; predictive analytics for risk; family search and engagement; mobile caseworker tools

buyingTriggers.name: CDC Data Modernization Initiative (DMI) Funding
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CDC DMI grant award to state; ELR mandate; syndromic surveillance expansion requirement
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: Grant award within 90 days; implementation 12-36 months
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Surveillance system modernization; ELR interfaces; analytics platform; cloud infrastructure

buyingTriggers.name: 988 Crisis Lifeline Expansion Grant
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: SAMHSA 988 grant award; state crisis system mandate; mobile crisis team scaling requirement
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Crisis call center platform; mobile dispatch; behavioral health EHR; data reporting to SAMHSA

buyingTriggers.name: HUD PHA Modernization / MTW Expansion
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: PHA selected for Moving to Work (MTW) expansion; HCV waitlist crisis; RAD conversion program
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: PHA ERP replacement; landlord portal; inspection mobile app; waitlist management system

buyingTriggers.name: Olmstead Plan Enforcement or Settlement
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS issues Olmstead compliance notice; DOJ settlement; state consent decree for community integration
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: Immediate upon notice; 12-36 month compliance window
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: HCBS case management; diversion assessment tools; housing navigation; employment supports platform

buyingTriggers.name: Federal Health and Human Services Grant (NoFO)
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS, ACF, SAMHSA, or ACL releases NOFO with technology requirements; new block grant guidance
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Grant-funded technology procurement; compliance-driven requirements; federal approval process (APD for Medicaid)

buyingTriggers.name: State HHS Agency Consolidation
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Governor mandates agency merger; shared services initiative; IT consolidation across health and human services
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Enterprise integration platform; unified eligibility system; data warehouse; cross-program case management

buyingTriggers.name: Federal ACA / Marketplace Special Enrollment Period
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS opens special enrollment period; state-based marketplace upgrade required; eligibility system integration mandate
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Eligibility and enrollment system scaling; marketplace interface; navigator support tools

buyingTriggers.name: Public Health Emergency Declaration / Pandemic Response
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: HHS declares public health emergency; state activates emergency operations center; federal emergency funding released
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Emergency contact tracing; vaccine management; surveillance surge; telehealth expansion; crisis services

personas.name: Medicaid Director / State Medicaid Director (SMD)
personas.role: Leads State Medicaid Agency; oversees $1B-$50B program; manages CMS federal-state partnership, waivers, and MCO contracts
personas.seniority: Executive / Cabinet-level appointee or SES equivalent
goals: Maintain CMS compliance and federal matching
Optimize MCO value and network adequacy

personas.name: Public Health Officer / State Epidemiologist
personas.role: Leads state or local health department; oversees disease surveillance, outbreak response, environmental health, and health promotion
pressures: Staff vacancies in epidemiology and lab
trustedEvidence: Peer state health department case studies
dislikedClaims: Solutions that don't work in outbreak conditions
Vendor hype without public health validation

personas.name: Child Welfare Case Manager Supervisor
personas.role: Supervises frontline child welfare staff; manages caseloads, safety assessments, placement decisions, and court documentation
pressures: High staff turnover and training burden
dislikedClaims: Systems that don't reflect field reality
Solutions requiring caseworkers to be data entry clerks

personas.name: Unemployment Insurance Claims Director
personas.role: Leads UI claims processing, adjudication, overpayment recovery, and trust fund administration; reports to state labor agency head
pressures: DOL ETA performance targets and sanctions
dislikedClaims: Fraud solutions that deny legitimate claimants

personas.name: Eligibility Worker Supervisor
personas.role: Supervises eligibility determination staff across Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, and other means-tested programs; manages workflow, quality, and training
dislikedClaims: Automation that eliminates jobs without notice

personas.name: Behavioral Health Program Director
personas.role: Leads state or county behavioral health authority; manages SAMHSA block grants, Medicaid behavioral health benefits, crisis systems, and provider network
goals: Expand access to mental health and SUD services
Integrate behavioral health with primary care
Reduce ED and justice system diversion
pressures: Medicaid MCO behavioral health carve-out complexity

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current Medicaid eligibility determination time, and how does it compare to the 45-day federal maximum?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Baseline eligibility processing performance; identify backlog severity

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your Medicaid renewals are completed through ex parte (automated) processes vs. requiring member action?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess automation maturity and churn risk

discoveryQuestions.question: How many separate eligibility and case management systems do your caseworkers access daily across Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, and child care programs?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify system fragmentation and integration opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current SNAP QC error rate, and has USDA issued any corrective action or enhanced oversight notices?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Identify federal compliance risk and funding exposure

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your first payment timeliness rate for unemployment insurance claims, and how has it trended since the pandemic?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Baseline UI processing performance; identify modernization need

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently verify claimant identity, and what is the average time to resolve identity holds?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess fraud prevention vs. service delivery balance

discoveryQuestions.question: How many systems does a child welfare caseworker need to access to complete a single safety assessment or placement decision?
What is your AFCARS data quality rate, and have you had any federal compliance findings in the past two submission cycles?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess federal compliance posture for child welfare

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your notifiable disease reports are submitted electronically vs. via fax or phone, and what is the average reporting delay?
How many EDs in your jurisdiction are connected to syndromic surveillance, and what are the data quality gaps?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess outbreak detection coverage and readiness

discoveryQuestions.question: What is the average wait time for non-crisis outpatient behavioral health services in your network, and how has it changed in the past year?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify access gap and provider shortage severity

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your 988 crisis line answer rate, and do you have mobile crisis teams dispatched through the same system?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess crisis system integration and performance

discoveryQuestions.question: How many households are currently on your Housing Choice Voucher waitlist, and what is the average time from application to voucher issuance?
What is your current MMIS vendor contract end date, and has CMS assessed your MITA maturity level in the past two years?
How do you currently track and manage HCBS waiver waitlists, and what percentage of individuals on the waitlist are diverted to institutional settings?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Assess Olmstead compliance and community integration capacity

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your eligibility worker annual turnover rate, and what is the average time for a new worker to process cases independently?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Quantify workforce crisis and training burden

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently share data between your state veterans benefits agency and federal VA systems or county veterans service officers?
What is your CCDF provider reimbursement cycle time, and what percentage of providers receive payment within 30 days of submission?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryGoal: Identify child care subsidy delivery efficiency

objections.name: CMS Approval Timeline Risk
objections.objectionText: Any Medicaid system change requires CMS Advanced Planning Document (APD) approval, which takes 12-18 months. We can't move fast enough.
objections.responseStrategy: Reference modular APD pathways (90-day review for modular components); highlight states with successful fast-track modular MMIS approvals; offer CMS relationship support
sources: State modular MMIS examples (e.g., Illinois, Vermont)

objections.name: Federal Funding Uncertainty
objections.objectionText: Federal match rates and grant guidance change with each administration. We can't commit to multi-year technology investments.
objections.responseStrategy: Emphasize modular, incremental approaches; highlight FMAP entitlements as statutory; reference 90/10 match for Medicaid eligibility/IT; offer flexible contracting terms

objections.name: Worker Union Opposition to Automation
objections.objectionText: Our eligibility worker union opposes any technology that reduces headcount or changes job classifications.
objections.responseStrategy: Frame as worker empowerment (reducing data entry, increasing client-facing time); reference AFSCME/SEIU collaborative modernization successes; offer labor-management partnership model
sources: Code for America integrated benefits research

objections.name: Legacy System Integration Complexity
objections.objectionText: Our MMIS/eligibility system is 20+ years old and deeply intertwined with downstream systems. Rip-and-replace is too risky.
objections.responseStrategy: Propose modular, phased replacement (CMS MITA modular architecture); offer API wrapper/adapter layer; reference successful brownfield modernization patterns

objections.name: Data Sharing Privacy Constraints
objections.objectionText: We can't share eligibility data across programs due to HIPAA, 42 CFR Part 2, and state privacy laws.
objections.responseStrategy: Reference CMS streamlined verification authority; highlight privacy-preserving integration patterns (data minimization, role-based access, audit trails); cite states with successful cross-program data sharing (MN, KY)

objections.name: Procurement Cycle Too Long
objections.objectionText: Our state procurement process takes 18-24 months for IT systems. By the time we implement, requirements will have changed.
objections.responseStrategy: Reference modular SaaS procurements that fit under simplified thresholds; agile contracting vehicles; cooperative purchasing agreements (NASPO, Western States Contracting Alliance)

objections.name: Pandemic Funding Cliff
objections.objectionText: We used ARPA/COVID relief funds for temporary staff and systems. Now that funding is ending, we can't sustain these solutions.
objections.responseStrategy: Focus on permanent efficiency gains (ex parte automation, churn reduction) that reduce ongoing administrative costs; demonstrate FMAP savings that offset state share; reference maintenance of effort protections

objections.name: Veterans Services Are Federal Responsibility
objections.objectionText: Veterans benefits are a federal VA responsibility. Our state agency just does advocacy and referral.
objections.responseStrategy: Highlight state veterans homes, employment programs, and mental health navigation as state-funded; reference federal-state partnership models; cite HUD-VASH and SSVF coordination gaps

objections.name: Behavioral Health Provider Shortage Can't Be Solved by Technology
objections.objectionText: We don't need software—we need more psychiatrists and therapists. Technology won't create providers.
objections.responseStrategy: Frame as provider productivity and reach expansion (telehealth, e-prescribing, collaborative care models); reference workforce pipeline technology (loan repayment tracking, licensure reciprocity tools); highlight crisis diversion that reduces demand on scarce providers

technologySystems.name: Medicaid Management Information System (MMIS)
technologySystems.description: Medicaid claims processing, provider enrollment, prior authorization, and payment. Legacy systems often 20+ years old.

technologySystems.name: Eligibility and Enrollment System (E&E)
technologySystems.description: Determines eligibility for Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, CHIP, and other means-tested programs. Often siloed by program.

technologySystems.name: Child Welfare Case Management System (CCWIS / SACWIS)
technologySystems.description: Tracks child welfare cases from intake through permanency. Federal CCWIS standards replacing SACWIS.

technologySystems.name: Unemployment Insurance Benefits System
technologySystems.description: Processes UI claims, wage verification, adjudication, and payments. Often integrated with tax systems.

technologySystems.name: SNAP EBT and Eligibility System
technologySystems.description: SNAP eligibility determination, case management, and Electronic Benefit Transfer card management.

technologySystems.name: Disease Surveillance and Electronic Lab Reporting (ELR)
technologySystems.description: Notifiable disease tracking, outbreak detection, and electronic laboratory result reporting to public health agencies.

technologySystems.name: Immunization Information System (IIS)
technologySystems.description: Registry of immunization doses administered; supports vaccine inventory, recall, and cross-jurisdictional sharing.

technologySystems.name: Behavioral Health EHR and Crisis Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Electronic health records for mental health and SUD treatment; crisis call center and mobile crisis dispatch.

technologySystems.name: Housing Authority Management System (PHAMS / HAP)
technologySystems.description: Voucher administration, HQS inspection tracking, landlord engagement, and waitlist management.

technologySystems.name: Aging and Disability Services Case Management (ADRC / No Wrong Door)
technologySystems.description: Aging and Disability Resource Center systems; long-term care options counseling; HCBS waiver management.

technologySystems.name: Child Care Subsidy and CCDF Administration System
technologySystems.description: CCDF eligibility, provider enrollment, attendance tracking, and quality rating integration.

technologySystems.name: Cross-Program Integrated Eligibility and Data Warehouse
technologySystems.description: Unified eligibility platform spanning Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, child care, housing, and other programs.

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Medicaid Managed Care Final Rule (42 CFR 438)
regulatoryFactors.description: Updated requirements for network adequacy, encounter data, rate setting, and quality oversight. States must demonstrate compliance by 2024-2026.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Federal funding reduction; CMS corrective action; MCO contract sanctions

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Medicaid Eligibility and Enrollment Streamlined Verification
regulatoryFactors.description: Requires states to use electronic data sources and ex parte processes to verify eligibility and reduce beneficiary burden.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: CMS CMP; FMAP reduction; corrective action plan

regulatoryFactors.name: SNAP Quality Control and Federal Funding Share
regulatoryFactors.description: USDA FNS mandates QC error rate <6%. States above threshold face enhanced oversight and potential federal funding share reduction.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Federal funding share reduction from 50% to 25%; corrective action requirements

regulatoryFactors.name: DOL ETA UI Performance Targets and Trust Fund Solvency
regulatoryFactors.description: DOL ETA sets timeliness and accuracy targets for UI claims processing. States below threshold face administrative funding sanctions.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Administrative grant reduction; enhanced oversight; technical assistance mandate

regulatoryFactors.name: Child Welfare CCWIS / AFCARS Compliance
regulatoryFactors.description: HHS ACF mandates Comprehensive Child Welfare Information System (CCWIS) standards and AFCARS data quality. States face title IV-E funding penalties for noncompliance.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Title IV-E funding penalty; CFSR compliance action; PIP requirements

regulatoryFactors.name: CDC Data Modernization Initiative (DMI) and ELR Mandate
regulatoryFactors.description: CDC requires states to modernize surveillance with electronic lab reporting, case management, and outbreak response capabilities.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: PHEP grant reduction; ELC grant conditions; public health emergency response gaps

regulatoryFactors.name: SAMHSA Block Grant Requirements and 988 Implementation
regulatoryFactors.description: SAMHSA requires states to report on block grant outcomes, maintain 988 crisis services, and meet National Suicide Prevention Lifeline standards.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Block grant funding reduction; technical assistance; peer review

regulatoryFactors.name: HUD PHA Performance and Voucher Utilization Requirements
regulatoryFactors.description: HUD requires PHAs to maintain lease-up rates >95%, complete HQS inspections timely, and administer vouchers within HUD standards.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Administrative fee reduction; voucher reallocation; MTW suspension

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Olmstead Community Integration Enforcement
regulatoryFactors.description: CMS enforces Olmstead integration mandate through state plan reviews, waivers, and DOJ coordination. States must demonstrate HCBS capacity.
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: CMS compliance action; DOJ settlement; consent decree; waiver suspension

regulatoryFactors.name: CCDF Reauthorization and Child Care Compliance
regulatoryFactors.description: HHS ACF CCDF reauthorization requires states to maintain eligibility determination timeliness, provider health/safety standards, and quality rating.
regulatoryFactors.effectiveDate: 2014 reauthorization; state plan updates every 3 years
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: CCDF funding reduction; state plan disapproval; monitoring findings

competitorFactors.name: Incumbent MMIS Vendor Entrenchment
competitorFactors.description: Legacy MMIS vendors (Gainwell, DXC, CNSI) hold long-term contracts with deep Medicaid program knowledge. Replacements face high switching costs.
competitorFactors.differentiationStrategy: Modular, cloud-native architecture; real-time analytics; faster implementation; lower TCO

competitorFactors.name: Large Systems Integrator Dominance in Eligibility
competitorFactors.description: Systems integrators (Deloitte, Accenture, Maximus) dominate state eligibility RFPs with end-to-end proposals and federal relationships.
competitorFactors.differentiationStrategy: Specialized vertical expertise; pre-built accelerators; agile delivery; state staff enablement

competitorFactors.name: Open Source and State-Developed Solutions
competitorFactors.description: Some states (e.g., Vermont, Michigan) have developed open-source MMIS or eligibility components. Shared services consortia emerging.
competitorFactors.differentiationStrategy: Commercial support; proven scalability; CMS pre-approval; faster deployment than custom build

competitorFactors.name: SaaS Case Management Disruption
competitorFactors.description: Cloud-native case management vendors (Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics, Appian) entering public health/human services with low-code platforms.
competitorFactors.differentiationStrategy: Pre-built vertical workflows; regulatory compliance templates; integration with federal systems

workedExamples.name: Medicaid Ex Parte Renewal Automation Value Model
workedExamples.scenario: State Medicaid agency with 1.2M enrollees processes 300K renewals annually. Baseline ex parte rate is 25%, manual renewal costs $35 each, and churn rate is 18%.
workedExamples.formulaApplied: PH-VF-002 (Ex Parte Renewal Efficiency) + PH-VF-001 (Churn Cost Avoidance)

result.valueCategory: Cost Savings + Mission Effectiveness + Risk Reduction
result.confidenceRationale: Based on state-reported averages; actual values vary by state administrative cost structure and churn composition

sensitivityAnalysis.lowCase: $22M (ex parte only reaches 55%, churn reduces to 12%)
sensitivityAnalysis.highCase: $48M (ex parte reaches 80%, churn reduces to 7%, gap-in-care costs higher)

workedExamples.name: Child Welfare Case Management Consolidation Value Model
workedExamples.scenario: State child welfare agency operates 8 separate case management systems for CPS, foster care, adoption, and family services. 500 caseworkers spend 15 hours/week on data entry across systems.
calculationSteps: Step 2: Caseworker Hours Saved = 500 workers * 10 hrs/week * 48 weeks * $45 = $10.8M
Step 3: Recurrence Reduction = $2M (from better case history visibility)

result.valueCategory: Cost Savings + Risk Reduction + Mission Effectiveness
result.confidenceRationale: Hours saved depends on unified system usability; recurrence reduction requires longitudinal outcome tracking

sensitivityAnalysis.highCase: $22M (15 hrs saved, 20% recurrence reduction from predictive analytics)

workedExamples.name: SNAP Error Rate Reduction and Federal Funding Protection
workedExamples.scenario: State SNAP agency with $600M annual outlay has QC error rate of 8.5% (above 6% threshold). Federal funding share is 50%. State must reduce error rate to 4% to avoid enhanced oversight.

result.confidenceRationale: USDA SNAP QC data is standardized; federal funding share formula is statutory; calculation is deterministic

sensitivityAnalysis.lowCase: $10M (error rate only reduces to 5.5%, partial corrective action still needed)
sensitivityAnalysis.highCase: $21M (error rate reduces to 3%, full federal share restored with penalty reversal)

22 Vertical KPIs (Medicaid eligibility time, ex parte rate, churn, SNAP QC error, SNAP timeliness, CPS response, AFCARS quality, maltreatment recurrence, UI first payment, UI IP rate, identity resolution, disease reporting, ELR coverage, BH wait time, 988 answer rate, BH-PC integration, voucher lease-up, HQS pass rate, MCO encounter completeness, network adequacy, HCBS waitlist, ADRC resolution)

12 Vertical Value Drivers (Eligibility backlog, SNAP QC, Child welfare fragmentation, UI backlog, Surveillance latency, BH access crisis, Housing utilization, MCO oversight, HCBS pressure, Worker retention, MMIS EOL, Cross-program silos)

12 Vertical Formulas (Medicaid churn cost avoidance, Ex parte efficiency, SNAP error reduction, Child welfare consolidation, UI fraud reduction, Surveillance modernization, BH access expansion, Housing throughput, MCO encounter improvement, HCBS waitlist reduction, Worker retention, Cross-program integration)

18 Vertical Benchmarks (Medicaid eligibility time, improper payment, SNAP error, UI timeliness, CPS response, maltreatment recurrence, BH wait time, 988 answer rate, voucher lease-up, HQS pass rate, MCO encounter, HCBS waitlist, disease reporting, ELR coverage, worker turnover, MMIS adjudication)

18 Vertical Signal Rules (Medicaid backlog surge, SNAP QC above threshold, Child welfare fragmentation, UI fraud backlog, Surveillance latency, BH access crisis, Housing voucher decline, MCO oversight gaps, HCBS/Olmstead pressure, Worker retention crisis, MMIS EOL, Cross-program silos, Emergency preparedness, IIS fragmentation, TANF rigidity, CSE lag, CCDF bottleneck, Veterans coordination gap)

14 Vertical Buying Triggers (Medicaid unwinding, SNAP QC penalty, CMS 438 rule, MMIS recompete, UI modernization, Child welfare RFP, CDC DMI funding, 988 expansion, HUD PHA modernization, Olmstead enforcement, Federal HHS NOFO, State HHS consolidation, ACA enrollment period, Public health emergency)

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Subpack provides more granular churn segmentation (Medicaid-specific, SNAP-specific, program-specific drivers) and adds ex parte renewal rate as companion KPI
Subpack decomposes into 5+ specific pains (eligibility backlog, MCO oversight, MMIS EOL, cross-program silos, worker retention) with program-specific symptoms and KPIs
Subpack refines with post-unwinding state-specific variables (gap-in-care cost, CMS compliance avoidance) and adds ex parte renewal efficiency formula

verticalPersonaAdditions: PH-PERS-001: Medicaid Director / State Medicaid Director (SMD) — CMS-focused, federal-state partnership, MCO oversight, MMIS governance
PH-PERS-002: Public Health Officer / State Epidemiologist — surveillance-focused, outbreak response, CDC relationship, data modernization
PH-PERS-003: Child Welfare Case Manager Supervisor — frontline child safety, AFCARS compliance, court timelines, worker wellbeing
PH-PERS-004: Unemployment Insurance Claims Director — DOL compliance, trust fund solvency, fraud detection, claimant experience
PH-PERS-005: Eligibility Worker Supervisor — means-tested program operations, federal QC compliance, worker retention, client service
PH-PERS-006: Behavioral Health Program Director — SAMHSA block grants, crisis systems, provider network, justice diversion
verticalKPIExtensions: PH-KPI-001: Medicaid Eligibility Determination Time (program-specific, not generic case processing)
PH-KPI-002: Ex Parte Renewal Rate (Medicaid/SNAP specific, not generic digital adoption)
PH-KPI-004: SNAP QC Error Rate (USDA-specific quality control, not generic improper payment)
PH-KPI-006: CPS Response Time (statutory child safety threshold, not generic emergency response)
PH-KPI-007: AFCARS Data Quality Rate (federal child welfare compliance, not generic data quality)
PH-KPI-009: UI First Payment Timeliness (DOL ETA specific, not generic backlog)
PH-KPI-012: Notifiable Disease Reporting Timeliness (CDC specific, not generic response time)
PH-KPI-013: ELR Coverage (public health surveillance specific)
PH-KPI-015: 988 Crisis Call Answer Rate (SAMHSA specific behavioral health crisis)
PH-KPI-019: MCO Encounter Data Completeness (Medicaid managed care specific)
PH-KPI-021: HCBS Waiver Waitlist (disability/aging community integration specific)
